title: A/B sample — is the two-seed a fraud?

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: No no no, you do not get to hang a two-seed banner for beating three teams with losing records. You don't!

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: [sighs] Here we go. Max, they have the second-best point differential in the conference. That is not a schedule artifact.

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: Point differential? POINT differential? Who hangs a point differential banner? Where does it go, the analytics department?

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: [chuckles] The teams that understand it tend to hang the real ones later. But sure, let's do feelings instead.

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: Okay, feelings? Feelings?! I watched that game Saturday. Fourth quarter, down six, and their star is standing in the corner like a mailbox—

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: A mailbox that scored thirty-one points, Max.

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: In garbage time! Half of that came when the game was already gone. You know this. You KNOW this.

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: I know that thirty-one is thirty-one. [pause] Look — I'll give you this much. The rotation gets thin late in games. That part is real.

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: Thank you! Was that so hard?

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: It was, actually. [laughs softly] Physically painful.

lines.speaker: Max Voltage
lines.text: There it is, folks. Growth. Alright — playoffs start Friday, and if they lose in round one, I don't want to hear a word about point differential.

lines.speaker: Dr. Linebreak
lines.text: And if they win it all, I expect that apology in writing. Notarized.
